results.title: Image 3 of Detroit evening times (Detroit, Mich), October 18, 1942, (FINAL)
results.description: Sunday October 18 1942 Detroit Welcomes Red Girl Sniper H Photo by Tiitm Staff Photograph r LT LUDMILA FAVLICHENKO pictured in Detroit Crowd Honor 4 Other United Nation Veteran A auburn haired girl wearing the full regalia and most of the decorations of the Russian army stood with four others on the steps of city hall Saturday to receive a formal welcome to Detroit The announcer had trouble with her name It didn t sound much like Lt Liudmila Pavhrhenko 1 as it came through the loud speaker But the crowd of 300 in the plaza recognized her They had cheered the others For the 26yearold woman who Is credited with killing 309 Nazis however they resfrved their heaviest volume Company of Heroes It was aeveral minutes before the sombereyed Lieutenant Pavli chenko could through an inter preter respond with thanks Although the Soviet heroine dominated the proceedings in the minds of her audience she by no means monopolized the valor rep resented by the small band of visi tors Introduced by Mayor Edward J Jeffrie Jr were Lt Vladimir Pchelintsev whose chief regret is that he wasted two bullets in slay ing 152 German soldiers during the defense of

results.title: Image 5 of Evening star (Washington, D.C.), July 17, 1944
results.description: Johnston Warns U S Against Letup in Pace Of War Production the Auocialed Press Eric Johnston president of the United States Chamber of Com merce warns against any letup in the pace set by industry agri culture and labor to meet the challenge of our enemies Declaring that for the first time in five years hope exists that the end of the war is in sight Mr Johnston said that only such a slackening can delay ultimate victory His statement was issued yester day along with an 11point program outlined by the chamber to make fully effective the dynamic forces of American business toward short ening the war and preparing for peacetime production The chamber said business will devote its energies toward winning the war quickly and will embrace policies under which service men and women can reenter civilian life with profitable employment Speedy Reconversion Favored The organization also went on record in favor of reconversion to peacetime production as rapidly as the war permits and in obtaining speedy settlement of terminated war contracts Mr Johnston also said that the problems which the wars end will bring are beginning to be clearly outlined Through the program the chamber is sponsoring

results.title: Image 32 of Evening star (Washington, D.C.), February 23, 1941
results.description: t PART TWO EDITORIAL SECTION v Editorial PageFeatures OrganizationsCivics TWELVE PAGES WASHINGTON I C FEBRUARY 23 1941 _ Aid Measure Expected to Link America Closely With Britain Economic Defense Department Held Possible Observer Doubts Enactment Would Drag This Country Into War By Felix Morley It Is now generally realized that the approaching enactment of the leaselend bill will open a new chapter in American history But it is much easier to recog nize that fact than it is to estimate what the fact portends Various historic principles and as sumptions will certainly be laid to rest when the President affixes his signature to this bill even as amended The doc trine of isolation with all that is implied therein will be a thing of the past The same will be true of the concept of American neutrality in foreign wars For with acceptance of the doctrine that it is our duty to restrict aggression anywhere no future war will be for eign to the United States In theory at least the armed strength of this country should henceforth be at the disposal of whatever organization may be established to preserve peace This is the second time that executive effort has

results.title: Image 16 of Evening star (Washington, D.C.), July 17, 1944
results.description: SOCIETY AND GENERAL NEWS WASHINGTON D C WASHINGTON AND VICINITY MONDAY JULY 17 1944 Newcomer Heads Lisl In Treasury Report On 1942 Salaries Bj the Associated Press A newcomer Sidney R Fleisher took first place with 645000 in the first edition of the Treasurys 1942 salary list out today The report which does not list income from capital or investments includes individuals who received 75000 or more from corporations for personal services for the calen dar year 1942 or corporation fiscal years ending in 1943 The internal revenue code requires its issuance The figures listed are gross re ceipts before the payment of Fed eral and State income taxes which would reduce materially the net amounts received Mr Fleisher received the money from 20th CenturyFox Film Corp He replaces temporarily at least L B Mayer the movie magnate at the head of the list Other Compensation The entire amount Daid Mr Fleisher was listed as other com pensation with no designations as to salary bonus or commissions Mr Mayer who topped last years list with 94976584 in salary and other compensation from Loews Inc was not included in todays list Treasury officials explained however that supplementary lists are issued annually

results.title: Image 4 of Imperial Valley press (El Centro, Calif.), February 17, 1943
results.description: 4 Editorials and Comment A FAIR DEAL FOR NONUNION WORKERS Imperial Valley should unite to place nonunion worker representatives on any and all boards or committees provided in the war effort where labor representa tion is provided by regulations We have specific reference to the re cently appointed appeal committee l the local area war manpower program Under the regulations this committee must bi composed of equal representa tives of labor and management This pro vision has been carried out properly in the selection of the local committee RUT all of labors representatives have lxen selected from the ranks of organiz ed workers and none from the ranks of nonunion employes This is completely out of line with the worker situation in Imperial county where it is generally believed for every union man or woman there are at least five nonunion employes Whatever the ratio may be certain it is that the division of union and non union members of the committee should be in proportion to the best estimate of union and nonunion people employed in the community This is not a personal attack upon any of the men recently announced as being appointed to the committee Some we
